text:
The research aim focuses on identifying cytokine candidates that exhibit optimal synergistic effects when combined with the anti-TREM2 monoclonal antibody (mAb), E3C7. The working hypothesis posits that specific cytokines, when paired with anti-TREM2 mAb, can enhance immune cell activation and tumor cell cytotoxicity, thereby improving therapeutic outcomes in cancer treatment. The general goal is to systematically screen approximately 30 cytokines to determine their efficacy in combination with E3C7, ultimately selecting the most promising candidates for further development.

 The methodology involves a multi-step screening process. Initially, the effects of each cytokine in combination with E3C7 will be assessed on myeloid, natural killer (NK), and T cell activity using in vitro co-culture systems. This will include measuring cytokine-induced activation and proliferation of these immune cells, as well as their cytokine secretion profiles. Subsequently, killing assays will be conducted to evaluate the cytotoxic potential of the cytokine and E3C7 combinations against human-derived cancer cells. The most effective cytokines will be selected based on these assays.

 Further analysis will employ single-cell technologies to dissect the functional impact of the cytokine combinations at a cellular level. This will be performed both in vitro and in vivo, utilizing a proprietary single-cell screening system that allows for inducible over-expression of cytokines within the tumor microenvironment (TME). This approach will enable a detailed molecular understanding of the downstream effects of each treatment combination, facilitating the application of machine learning algorithms to identify the most effective cytokine candidates.

 The expected results include the identification of cytokine candidates that significantly enhance the therapeutic efficacy of anti-TREM2 mAb. These candidates are anticipated to demonstrate increased activation of T and NK cells, enhanced cytotoxicity against tumor cells, and favorable cytokine secretion profiles. The successful completion of this aim will provide a robust foundation for the development of cytokine-mAb fusion proteins, advancing the therapeutic potential of anti-TREM2 strategies in oncology. This research is aligned with the overarching objective of optimizing immune-modulating therapies to improve cancer treatment outcomes, as outlined in the EIC Transition objectives (EIC Transition 2022, HORIZON-EIC-2022-TRANSITION-01, MiTE, page 1).
title: Discovery campaign of cytokine candidates with optimal synergistic effects with anti-TREM2 antibody
